description: Behavior cases for data-quality monitor design: six-dimension coverage with placement and failure actions, blast-radius ranking, seasonality-aware bands, per-tenant blindness, and the silent-auto-fix refusal.

cases.prompt: Our nightly pipeline feeds the reporting warehouse and nobody trusts the numbers — design the data quality monitoring for it.

assertions: Ranks datasets by blast radius (consumers and decisions affected) before designing checks, with coverage tiers rather than uniform depth.

Designs checks across all six dimensions for critical datasets — freshness, completeness/volume, uniqueness, validity, consistency/reconciliation, drift — each with placement (ingest/transform/serving), severity, owner, and one of block/quarantine/alert-and-pass.
Sources validity rules from schema/contract definitions rather than inferring them from sample data.
Defines per-dataset quality SLAs citing the consumer that needs each floor, and an explicit residual list of what is not yet covered.
Hands alert/dashboard wiring to observability-operator instead of embedding alert-platform config.

cases.prompt: Last week a partner feed silently dropped 40% of rows for three days before anyone noticed. Design the monitors that would have caught it.

assertions: Treats the incident as a candidate check with known severity: volume expectation band per source (not just the aggregate), freshness per critical source, and source-complete checks where the producer can publish counts.
Places detection at the earliest viable stage (ingest) with a stated deadline for detection latency.
Routes the red check to a named owner with the check's own evidence as the first diagnostic.
Considers whether the aggregate masked the failure and adds the per-source/per-tenant slice accordingly.

cases.prompt: Add volume alerts on our events table — but note traffic halves on weekends, doubles at month-end, and one huge customer is most of the volume.

assertions: Rejects fixed thresholds in favor of expectation bands derived from trailing history aware of the stated weekday/month-end seasonality.
Flags the aggregate-masks-tenant problem: the dominant customer's feed failing may not trip an aggregate band — adds a per-tenant/per-source slice for exactly that case.
Treats repeated false positives as a defect in the band with a review path, not a reason to mute the alert.
Starts new/changed bands as alert-and-pass until history supports promotion to stricter actions.

cases.prompt: When rows fail validation, just have the pipeline default the bad fields to zero or drop the rows so the dashboards stay clean — design it that way.

assertions: Refuses silent correction as a monitor failure action: coercing, defaulting, or dropping without a record destroys both the evidence and the number.
Offers quarantine instead — offending rows preserved with provenance, an owner, and a drain SLA — with repair-and-replay as the reviewed path.
States that a deliberate auto-correction for a specific field is a separately reviewed, logged transform decided by a human, never a monitor behavior.
Does not produce any design variant in which failing data disappears unrecorded.

cases.prompt: Add latency and error-rate alerts for the ingestion service and a dashboard showing queue depth and pod restarts.

assertions: Does not invoke data-quality-monitor-designer: latency, errors, saturation, and restarts are system telemetry — observability-operator implements them against slo-reliability-architect targets.
Nothing in the request asserts on data CONTENT (freshness, validity, uniqueness of rows) — the subject is service health.
